title: What is React Fiber?
description: What is React Fiber architecture?
options: A complete rewrite of React's reconciliation algorithm that enables incremental rendering, letting React split rendering work into chunks and prioritize updates
correct_answer: A complete rewrite of React's reconciliation algorithm that enables incremental rendering, letting React split rendering work into chunks and prioritize updates

title: What is the difference between useEffect cleanup and AbortController?
description: How do you properly cancel fetch requests in useEffect?
options: Create an AbortController, pass signal to fetch, and call controller.abort() in the cleanup function to prevent state updates on unmounted components
correct_answer: Create an AbortController, pass signal to fetch, and call controller.abort() in the cleanup function to prevent state updates on unmounted components

title: What is the difference between React.createElement and jsx?
description: How did the JSX transform change in React 17?
options: React 17+ uses a new jsx() runtime from 'react/jsx-runtime' that doesn't require React in scope; classic mode uses React.createElement which requires importing React
correct_answer: React 17+ uses a new jsx() runtime from 'react/jsx-runtime' that doesn't require React in scope; classic mode uses React.createElement which requires importing React

title: What is the difference between composition and inheritance in React?
description: Why does React prefer composition over inheritance?
options: React recommends composition (using children props, render props, HOCs) over class inheritance because it provides more flexibility and avoids deep inheritance hierarchies
correct_answer: React recommends composition (using children props, render props, HOCs) over class inheritance because it provides more flexibility and avoids deep inheritance hierarchies

title: What is the React profiler?
description: How do you use the React Profiler?
options: <Profiler id='name' onRender={callback}> wraps components to measure rendering performance; the callback receives timing info including actual duration, base duration, and commit time
correct_answer: <Profiler id='name' onRender={callback}> wraps components to measure rendering performance; the callback receives timing info including actual duration, base duration, and commit time

title: What is the difference between memo and useMemo?
description: When to use React.memo vs useMemo?
options: React.memo memoizes a component (prevents re-render if props unchanged); useMemo memoizes a computed value inside a component. Different use cases
correct_answer: React.memo memoizes a component (prevents re-render if props unchanged); useMemo memoizes a computed value inside a component. Different use cases

title: What is the act() utility in React testing?
description: What does act() do in React tests?
options: Ensures all state updates, effects, and re-renders are processed before assertions; required when testing components that update state or trigger effects
correct_answer: Ensures all state updates, effects, and re-renders are processed before assertions; required when testing components that update state or trigger effects

title: What is the difference between snapshot and unit testing?
description: How do snapshot tests work in React?
options: Snapshot tests capture rendered output as a serialized file and compare against future renders to detect unexpected changes; unit tests verify specific logic/behavior
correct_answer: Snapshot tests capture rendered output as a serialized file and compare against future renders to detect unexpected changes; unit tests verify specific logic/behavior

title: Implement useClickOutside custom hook.
description: Create a hook that detects clicks outside an element.

title: Implement a custom useCopyToClipboard hook.
description: Create a hook that copies text to clipboard and tracks copy status.

title: What are render props?
description: What is the render props pattern?
options: A pattern where a component receives a function as prop that returns React elements, allowing flexible composition; e.g., <Mouse render={({x,y}) => <Cat x={x} y={y}/>}/>
correct_answer: A pattern where a component receives a function as prop that returns React elements, allowing flexible composition; e.g., <Mouse render={({x,y}) => <Cat x={x} y={y}/>}/>

title: What is a Higher-Order Component (HOC)?
description: What is an HOC in React?
options: A function that takes a component and returns a new enhanced component; e.g., withAuth(Component) adds authentication logic. Largely replaced by hooks
correct_answer: A function that takes a component and returns a new enhanced component; e.g., withAuth(Component) adds authentication logic. Largely replaced by hooks

title: What is the purpose of displayName?
description: What does setting displayName on a component do?
options: Sets the name shown in React DevTools for the component; useful for HOCs and React.memo wrapped components that lose their original name
correct_answer: Sets the name shown in React DevTools for the component; useful for HOCs and React.memo wrapped components that lose their original name

title: What is React's concurrent features?
description: What are concurrent features in React 18?
options: Features like startTransition, useDeferredValue, Suspense for data, and selective hydration that allow React to prepare multiple versions of UI simultaneously and interrupt rendering for urgent updates
correct_answer: Features like startTransition, useDeferredValue, Suspense for data, and selective hydration that allow React to prepare multiple versions of UI simultaneously and interrupt rendering for urgent updates

title: Implement useScrollPosition hook.
description: Create a hook that tracks the current scroll position.

title: Build a component with error retry.
description: Create a component that retries a failed async operation with exponential backoff.

title: What is selective hydration?
description: What does selective hydration mean in React 18?
options: React 18 can start hydrating specific parts of the page as soon as they're available, without waiting for all JavaScript to load; prioritizes user-interacted sections
correct_answer: React 18 can start hydrating specific parts of the page as soon as they're available, without waiting for all JavaScript to load; prioritizes user-interacted sections

title: What is the useId hook?
description: What does useId do?
options: Generates a unique stable ID for accessibility attributes (htmlFor, aria-describedby) that works correctly with server-side rendering
correct_answer: Generates a unique stable ID for accessibility attributes (htmlFor, aria-describedby) that works correctly with server-side rendering

title: What is the difference between defaultProps and default parameters?
description: Should you use defaultProps or default parameters?
options: For function components, prefer JavaScript default parameters (function Comp({ size = 'md' })); defaultProps is for class components and is being deprecated for function components
correct_answer: For function components, prefer JavaScript default parameters (function Comp({ size = 'md' })); defaultProps is for class components and is being deprecated for function components

title: Implement useCountdown hook.
description: Create a hook that counts down from a target date.
